name: Oprah Winfrey
executiveSummary:
Oprah Winfrey is an American talk show host, television producer, actress, author, and media proprietor. She is best known for her globally syndicated talk show, The Oprah Winfrey Show, which was the highest-rated television program of its kind in history.

Dubbed the 'Queen of All Media', she became the first Black female billionaire in North America. Winfrey runs her own television network (OWN) and remains a highly influential philanthropist and cultural icon.

basicDetails.currentRole: Chairwoman & CEO, Harpo Productions; CEO, OWN

biography:
Winfrey was born into poverty in rural Mississippi to a teenage single mother. She was raised in Milwaukee and Tennessee. Despite extreme early hardships, she landed a job in radio while still in high school. By 19, she was a co-anchor for the local evening news in Nashville. Her emotional, extemporaneous delivery eventually led to her transfer to the daytime talk show arena.

She took over a failing local Chicago talk show, AM Chicago, in 1983, which quickly skyrocketed to first place and was renamed The Oprah Winfrey Show. It went into national syndication in 1986 and ran for 25 years. Through her production company, Harpo Productions, she retained ownership of the show, generating immense wealth.

In addition to her television empire, Winfrey is an Oscar-nominated actress, a prolific publisher (O, The Oprah Magazine), and the founder of the Oprah Winfrey Leadership Academy for Girls in South Africa. Her endorsements have massive cultural and economic impact, often termed 'The Oprah Effect'.

careerTimeline.organization: The Oprah Winfrey Show / Harpo Productions

education.degree: Bachelor of Arts in Speech and Performing Arts

recentNews.title: Weight Management Special
